name: Weighted Linear Summation with State-Dependent Attribute Weights (Algebraic)
description: Static algebraic multi-attribute utility model where overall option value is a weighted linear sum of attribute values. Each food resource is decomposed into two attributes: an immediate attribute (proximity-based desirability) and an abstract attribute (palatability/quality). Attribute weights are dynamically modulated by internal hunger state and learnable attentional allocations. Actions are selected via softmax over computed action values. Attention allocations update via reward prediction error, enabling the agent to learn which attribute dimension is more predictive of reward. Grounded in Rangel (2013) postulates P1 (weighted linear summation), P3 (attentional modulation of weights), and P5 (immediate vs. abstract attribute distinction).

variables.name: option_value
variables.description: Scalar subjective value of an option (resource), computed as weighted sum of attribute values. Proximate driver of action selection.

variables.name: immediate_attribute_value
variables.description: Attribute value for the immediate/proximity dimension. For movement actions: 1.0 - (manhattan_distance / max_distance), where max_distance = grid_width + grid_height - 2. For eat action on a resource: 1.0. For stay or actions with no reachable resource: 0.0.

variables.name: abstract_attribute_value
variables.description: Attribute value for the abstract/quality dimension. Mapped from the 'palatability' property of the nearest food resource targeted by the action, normalized to [-1, 1] via: a_abs = 2 * (palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1. If no resource is targeted, a_abs = 0.

variables.name: immediate_weight
variables.description: Effective weight for the immediate attribute, computed from attentional allocation and hunger state. Always in [0, 1] and sums with w_abs to 1.

variables.name: abstract_weight
variables.description: Effective weight for the abstract attribute, computed from attentional allocation and temporal discount. Always in [0, 1] and sums with w_imm to 1.

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Internal physiological drive state. Rises by eta each step, drops by R_food when eating. 0 = fully sated, 1 = maximally hungry.

variables.name: attention_immediate
variables.description: Attentional allocation to the immediate attribute. Learned via reward prediction error. Sum of alpha_imm + alpha_abs = 1.

variables.name: attention_abstract
variables.description: Attentional allocation to the abstract attribute. Complement of alpha_imm. Learned via reward prediction error.

variables.name: action_value
variables.description: Value of taking a specific action, derived from the option value of the resource it targets.

parameters.name: temporal_discount_for_abstract_attributes
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): abstract attributes are down-weighted under low control

parameters.name: attention_learning_rate
parameters.source: Standard reinforcement learning rate for attentional updates

parameters.name: minimum_attention
parameters.source: Prevents any attribute from being fully ignored (Rangel 2013: all attributes contribute)

rules.description: Compute effective attribute weights from hunger state and attentional allocation. Hunger amplifies the immediate attribute weight; the abstract attribute is discounted by delta. Weights are normalized to sum to 1.
rules.pseudocode:
unnorm_imm = alpha_imm * (1 + gamma_H * H_t)
unnorm_abs = alpha_abs * delta
Z = unnorm_imm + unnorm_abs
w_imm = unnorm_imm / Z
w_abs = unnorm_abs / Z

rules.description: Compute attribute values for each candidate action. Immediate attribute = proximity-based desirability. Abstract attribute = palatability-derived quality rating of the targeted resource.
Compute option value as weighted linear sum of attribute values.
Softmax action selection over action values.
rules.pseudocode:
for each action a:
  logit_a = beta * V_a
max_logit = max(logits)
for each action a:
  prob_a = exp(logit_a - max_logit)
prob = prob / sum(prob)
selected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=prob)

rules.description: Hunger state update. Hunger rises by eta each step and drops by R_food when eating. Clipped to [0, 1].
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if (action == 'eat' and last_action_result.consumed) else 0
H_t = clip(H_t + eta - R_food * ate, 0, 1)

rules.description: Attention allocation update via reward prediction error. Attributes that are more correlated with positive RPE gain attention. Attention floored at epsilon and renormalized.
rules.pseudocode:
RPE = reward - V_selected
alpha_imm = alpha_imm + lam * RPE * a_imm_selected
alpha_abs = alpha_abs + lam * RPE * a_abs_selected
alpha_imm = max(epsilon, alpha_imm)
alpha_abs = max(epsilon, alpha_abs)
total = alpha_imm + alpha_abs
alpha_imm = alpha_imm / total
alpha_abs = alpha_abs / total

decision_logic.description: Each simulation step: (1) Read perception to get position, food locations, and hunger. (2) Compute attribute weights from hunger and attention via R1. (3) For each of the 6 available actions, compute immediate and abstract attribute values via R2. (4) Compute action values via R3. (5) Select action via softmax (R4). After action execution: (6) Update hunger via R5. (7) Update attention allocations via R6 using the reward prediction error.
pseudocode: # R2 + R3: For each action, compute attribute values and action value
food_at_pos = [f for f in food_list if f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1]]
store selected action's V_a, a_imm, a_abs for update phase

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {'x': int, 'y': int, 'type': 'food', 'palatability': float}
perception_to_variables.food_palatability: resource['palatability'] for each resource in food_sources

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action -> ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 on successful consumption)

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger increases over time without eating, biasing the agent toward proximity-seeking (immediate attribute) behavior.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps with no food reachable -> assert H_t > 0.9; assert w_imm > w_abs

expected_behaviors.description: After eating, hunger drops and the agent becomes relatively more quality-sensitive (abstract attribute weight increases).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: eat food at step T -> assert H_{T+1} < H_T; assert w_abs at T+1 > w_abs at T (relative to w_imm)

expected_behaviors.description: Agent preferentially moves toward closer food (high immediate attribute) when very hungry.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set H_t = 0.95, place two foods: near (dist=1, low palatability) and far (dist=5, high palatability) -> agent more likely to move toward near food

expected_behaviors.description: Attention allocation shifts toward the attribute dimension that better predicts reward over time.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 200 steps in env where high-palatability food gives reward -> assert alpha_abs increases relative to initial value

expected_behaviors.description: Agent eats food when standing on it and hungry.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place agent on food, set H_t = 0.8 -> assert P(eat) is highest action probability

references: Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717-1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561
Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9(7), 545-556. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357
